GraceChangeDesignContext.Rationale: Preset model choices and coordination strategy are related deployment decisions, but runtime model inference would be brittle. One managed vv-controller should therefore receive one concrete work policy resolved from the startup configuration snapshot, while its durable base prompt retains only universal invariants. This minimizes context pollution and preserves backward-compatible configuration behavior.

Alternatives.Alternative: Hard-coded model or provider matching was rejected because aliases and capability assumptions change and would make behavior difficult to predict.
Separate profile-specific primary agents were rejected because they duplicate prompts, registration state, and workflow access rules.
Describing every profile and naming the active one was rejected because inactive alternatives still consume context and invite reinterpretation.

Tool disabling, permissions, task filtering, and session override state were rejected for the first version because explicit vv-execute classic mode must remain available and prompt behavior should be evaluated first.
A required schema v4 section was rejected because optional schema v3 fields provide the needed behavior without forced migration.

DeferredDecisions.Decision: Whether observed policy violations justify tool or task enforcement in a future version.
Whether profile-specific cost, latency, token, or cache telemetry is useful for preset tuning.
Whether built-in profile mappings should change as managed model behavior evolves.

Scenarios.Scenario: A high-capability single-session preset causes vv-controller to explore, investigate, plan, implement, and verify directly without receiving inactive implementation-loop instructions.

An explicit or materially valuable review launches independent reviewers, after which vv-controller validates findings and either fixes them directly during active implementation or reports findings only for review-only requests.
An explicit vv-execute invocation still presents inline and classic choices, and the selected skill mode governs that plan execution without persistent runtime override state.
An existing valid schema v3 configuration without orchestration remains valid and resolves to balanced until a preset or scoped set command writes an explicit profile.
A custom preset that changes model roles but omits orchestration preserves the current root profile and reports that result.
